PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-07-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kayla Grant appeared by telephone for Petitioner.

The Court raised the issue of overcoming the presumption of revocation. Ms. Grant stated that a copy of the will was found in the Decedent's brief case which she believes rebuts presumption of revocation because it was kept in a safe place and that the Decedent never mentioned to anyone that he had a will. Ms. Grant also stated that the only discussion she had with Ms. Allen was about a month ago when she asked why she had not received notice, that prior to that she did not know about Ms. Allen, that her Client does not believe Ms. Allen is a Child of the Decedent, and that the Decedent lived with her Client for forty years and had a child with her Client. In addition, Ms. Grant stated that the Decedent had four children from his previous marriage, that she gave notice to all of them and that Ms. Allen is not one of the four children. The Court denied the Third Amended Petition for Probate of Lost Will. The Court also informed Ms. Grant that she will have to file a new petition for Letters of administration based on intestacy.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-24-2018 Continued To Jan-07-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kayla Grant, for and with Petitioner Beulah Smith.

Ms. Grant informed the Court that her declaration had incorrectly reported the finding of a duplicate original will, but that it was in face q photocopy rather than an original. She stated that there was another claimant, Adrienne Allen, who has filed a will contest but is not present today. Ms. Grant represented that the property to which Ms. Allen has asserted a claim is not part of the decedent's estate. It was Ms. Grant's understanding that she had been served with summons on the will contest, but that Ms. Allen had not served anyone else. The matter was taken under submission. At the request of Ms. Grant, the Court agreed that Petitioner Beulah Smith need not be present for the next hearing in this matter.

Subsequent to the hearing, Judge Stewart determined that a further hearing on the petition was needed, and that the matter was no longer under submission. The matter was continued to January 7, 2019 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Jonk K. Stewart; Clerk: Craig Blackstone; Bailiff Anasse. Not reported.
